prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. ARSAT-1 is a geostationary communications satellite operated by AR-SAT and built by the Argentine company INVAP. ARSAT-1 was launched into orbit on October 16, 2014, from French Guiana alongside Intelsat-30 satellite using an Ariane 5 rocket. It is expected to be located at 72° West longitude geostationary slot. ARSAT-1 is the first geostationary satellite built in Latin America. Total cost of the satellite is 270 million US dollars.ARSAT-1 carries a total of 24 IEEE Ku band (NATO J-band) transponders. Twelve will be operating at 36 MHz, eight at 54 MHz and four at 72 MHz, for a total bandwidth of 1152 MHz. It will offer a wide range of telecommunications, data transmission, telephone and television services mainly across all of Argentina, Chile, Uruguay and Paraguay including direct broadcast
B. The geostationary communications satellite known as ARSAT-1, developed by the Argentine firm INVAP, is under the operational control of AR-SAT. Its deployment into orbit occurred on October 16, 2014, originating from French Guiana, and was accomplished in conjunction with the Intelsat-30 satellite via an Ariane 5 rocket. The satellite is projected to occupy the geostationary orbital position of 72° West longitude. Notably, ARSAT-1 holds the distinction of being the inaugural geostationary satellite constructed within Latin America. The comprehensive expenditure for this satellite amounted to 270 million US dollars. ARSAT-1 is equipped with a complement of 24 IEEE Ku band (also referred to as NATO J-band) transponders. Specifically, twelve of these transponders will function at a frequency of 36 MHz, eight at 54 MHz, and four at 72 MHz, collectively providing a total bandwidth of 1152 MHz. This satellite is designed to furnish a broad spectrum of services, encompassing telecommunications, data transmission, telephony, and television, with its primary coverage extending across Argentina, Chile, Uruguay, and Paraguay, and including direct broadcast capabilities.
C. ARSAT-1, the first geostationary communications satellite in Latin America, was launched from French Guiana on October 16, 2014, using an Ariane 5 rocket. It is operated by AR-SAT and built by the Argentine company INVAP. It has a total cost of 270 million US dollars and includes 24 transponders, including a total bandwidth of 1152 MHz.

Answer:

Twelve will be operating at 36 MHz, eight at 54 MHz and four at 72 MHz, for a total bandwidth of 1152 MHz. It will offer a wide range of telecommunications, data transmission, telephone and television services mainly across all of Argentina, Chile, Uruguay and Paraguay including direct broadcast
